assumptions: The issue concerns consolidated financial statements.
The item in question is an intragroup dividend receivable/payable denominated in a foreign currency.
The question is about IFRS accounting models potentially relevant to hedging the foreign exchange exposure.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 39 in the context include cash flow hedge accounting and specifically address when foreign currency risk on intragroup transactions may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 39 in the context identify fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model relevant to foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 in the context provides the non-hedge accounting model for foreign currency monetary items and explains when intragroup monetary-item exchange differences remain in consolidated profit or loss or OCI.
